---
title: "Maritime Bushy Bluestem (Anatherum tenuispatheum)"
slug: "anatherum-tenuispatheum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475108172/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Maritime Bushy Bluestem"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475108172/medium.jpg" alt="Maritime Bushy Bluestem" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/billpranty">
billpranty</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264591018">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1616972)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common to coastal habitats, including maritime wet grasslands, salt marshes, and interdune swales.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its upright growth and narrow leaves, with airy, branching seed heads. In Florida, it is found in coastal and sandy habitats such as dunes and scrub. It grows primarily during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover and stabilization in native plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1616972-Anatherum-tenuispatheum)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/main.php?pg=show-taxon-detail.php&taxonid=1748)
